signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.34; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Implication: risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7161; 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew. Implication: late-trend caution signal as market hedging rather than chasing — watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD at 1.3521 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoC at 2.25% vs Fed at 3.75% — 150bp CAD carry disadvantage. USD/CAD at 1.3651, testing the 50-day moving average. Positioning neutral, no extreme reads. Implication: structurally capped, watch 1.3700 for resistance.
